seo.title: React Router v7 vs Prisma vs Drizzle
seo.description: A practical comparison of React Router v7, Prisma, and Drizzle for Remix.

content.intro: Comparing Remix and Next.js for full-stack React development, focusing on data workflows, deployment flexibility, and progressive enhancement trade-offs.

options.name: Remix
options.tagline: Full-stack framework with first-class data loading and progressive enhancement
options.best_for: Teams needing explicit data loading control, Cloudflare Workers deployment, and complex form workflows

options.name: Next.js
options.tagline: Hybrid static/server rendering framework with built-in API routes
options.best_for: Projects requiring SEO-first indexing, static site generation, and larger ecosystem tooling

criteria.name: Data Loading Patterns
criteria.description: How frameworks handle server-side data fetching and client-side updates

values.Remix: Loader functions with explicit data dependencies

criteria.name: Routing Complexity
criteria.description: Handling nested routes and shared data requirements

values.Remix: File-based routing with nested loader/action composition
values.Next.js: App directory routing with parallel routes and layout components

criteria.name: Deployment Targets
criteria.description: Supported runtime environments and edge computing capabilities

values.Next.js: Vercel, AWS, self-hosted Node.js, Edge Functions

criteria.name: Form Handling
criteria.description: Progressive enhancement support and optimistic UI patterns

values.Remix: Native form actions with built-in optimistic updates
values.Next.js: Requires custom implementation or libraries like react-hook-form

criteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
criteria.description: Available tools for database integration and testing

values.Next.js: TypeORM, Prisma, Playwright, and broader community libraries

criteria.name: Learning Curve
criteria.description: Time required to implement complex data workflows

values.Remix: Steeper initial curve for data loading architecture
values.Next.js: Easier onboarding with existing React patterns

criteria.name: Cost Profile
criteria.description: Infrastructure and tooling expenses for production

values.Remix: Lower cold start costs on Cloudflare Workers
values.Next.js: Higher Vercel costs for large-scale API routes

criteria.name: Lock-in Risk
criteria.description: Ease of migrating between frameworks or runtime environments

values.Remix: Higher risk due to framework-specific data loading model
values.Next.js: Lower risk with standard Node.js API routes

verdict.summary: Remix offers superior control over data flows and Cloudflare integration but requires more architectural investment. Next.js provides broader ecosystem support and easier onboarding for SEO-focused projects.

use_cases.scenario: Building a data-heavy CRM with progressive enhancement
use_cases.reason: Explicit data loading patterns reduce API complexity for nested forms and tables

use_cases.scenario: Creating an e-commerce site with SEO requirements
use_cases.reason: Static generation capabilities and existing SEO tooling provide faster time-to-market

use_cases.scenario: Deploying to Cloudflare Workers with minimal runtime costs
use_cases.reason: Native Worker support with optimized cold start performance

use_cases.scenario: Maintaining a large team with mixed React expertise
use_cases.reason: Familiar patterns reduce onboarding time for developers new to full-stack workflows
